direction: Extract knowledge as YAML graph + markdown natural language description, generate BSV from knowledge in isolated workspace
result: Generated code is functionally equivalent to original (manual review). Blocked on BSC compilation.
key_insight: truncateLSB name vs description conflict in knowledge. Generator followed function name over natural language description.

direction: Composite module with 2 library submodules in YAML graph. Knowledge cascade: System → Project → Top Graph + Library entries. Isolated agent with cwd parameter.
result: Generated code is functionally equivalent to original (manual review). Agent correctly wired both submodules, mkConnectionWithAction with deqAction, and PSN consumption.
key_insight: Composite modules with library-based submodule references work well. The graph connections + library YAML entries provide sufficient information for correct wiring. Variable naming differences don't affect correctness.

direction: Complex leaf module with 2-rule FSM, bit manipulation (zeroExtend, truncate, concatenation, left-shift), utility functions (calcHeaderInvalidFragByteAndBitNum, genByteEn), and immAssert checks.
result: Generated code compiled on first attempt. Functionally equivalent, minor cosmetic differences (package wrapper, notEmpty guards, equivalent utility function forms).
key_insight: Natural language descriptions of FSM state transitions and bit-level operations are sufficient for LLM to generate correct RTL. Dead code (unused variable) in original was correctly omitted.

direction: Most complex leaf module: 5-rule, 4-stage FSM with bit-level boundary merging. Two rounds: initial compilation used to identify knowledge gaps, then knowledge refined and regenerated.
result: Round 1: 2 compilation errors (immAssert arg count, type ambiguity). Round 2: compiled cleanly after fixing knowledge files.
key_insight: The compilation-feedback loop is essential. Knowledge must explicitly specify function argument counts, type annotation requirements (ByteEn not let for truncate), and exact package provenance of each function.
